[2022-09-01 00:34] Field of optometry has evolved to address vision care needs for patients of all ages
nodes.description: The field of optometry has evolved significantly to address vision care needs for patients of all ages through advanced technology, focus on preventive care, specialization, telemedicine, and patient education.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] Optometrists focus on preventive care for children and older adults
nodes.description: Optometrists have started focusing more on preventive care, especially for children and older adults, which includes regular eye examinations and vision screenings to detect any issues early on.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] Optometrists specialize in areas such as pediatric and geriatric optometry
nodes.description: Optometrists now specialize in different areas such as pediatric optometry, geriatric optometry, ocular disease, and vision therapy, allowing patients of all ages to seek specialized care for their unique vision care needs.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] Optometrists provide remote consultations through telemedicine
nodes.description: With the rise of telemedicine, optometrists are now able to provide remote consultations to patients who are unable to visit the clinic, making it easier for patients of all ages to receive timely and convenient eye care services.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] Optometrists educate patients on maintaining healthy vision
nodes.description: Optometrists now focus on educating patients of all ages on the importance of eye care and how to maintain healthy vision, including information on diet, lifestyle changes, and proper eye protection.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] Proper eye protection minimizes risk of eye injuries and diseases
nodes.description: Proper eye protection is crucial as it minimizes the risk of eye injuries and prevents damage caused by harmful UV rays, high-energy visible (HEV) blue light, and other environmental factors.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] Wearing safety glasses prevents eye injuries in various settings
nodes.description: Wearing appropriate eye protection, such as safety glasses or goggles, can help prevent injuries that can lead to vision loss in various settings, such as playing sports, working in hazardous environments, and participating in DIY projects.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] Sunglasses with UV protection reduce risk of eye diseases
nodes.description: Wearing sunglasses that offer UV protection and HEV blue light-blocking glasses can help reduce the risk of developing certain eye conditions such as cataracts, macular degeneration, and photokeratitis.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] Protective eyewear prevents irritation and inflammation
nodes.description: Protective eyewear, such as goggles or shields, can keep out dust, debris, and other small objects, preventing irritation or inflammation of the delicate structures of the eyes.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] User acknowledges the evolution of optometry in providing specialized care
nodes.description: User expresses gladness that optometry is evolving and providing specialized care for patients of all ages.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 00:34] User understands the importance of a holistic approach to eye care
nodes.description: User acknowledges that even with advanced technology, a holistic approach to eye care that addresses both the underlying causes of eye problems and helps to prevent further damage is essential.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Addis Ababa is ranked among countries with the worst air quality

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Spatial location of Addis Ababa contributes to air pollution

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Air Pollution Index (API) in Addis Ababa is calculated using specific pollutants
nodes.description: In Addis Ababa, the Air Pollution Index (API) is calculated using the concentration of carbon monoxide (CO), nitrogen dioxide (NO2), ozone (O3), sulphur dioxide (SO2), particulate matter (PM) with a diameter finer than 10 μm and PM2.5 (APIMS 2019).

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] PM10 from vehicles, biomass burning, and industrial emissions is a health threat in Addis Ababa
nodes.description: Despite the current use of PM2.5 to indicate the API level in Addis Ababa (APIMS 2020), PM10 sourced from vehicles' soot, biomass burning, and industrial emissions remains a threat to health (Khan et al., 2016) due to its small aerodynamic particulate diameter which can easily penetrate the lungs and respiratory system, resulting in adverse health effects (Tella and Balogun, 2021).

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Exposure to PM10 causes various health issues in Addis Ababa
nodes.description: The impact of exposure to PM10 has been discussed in several literature, causing cardiovascular diseases, respiratory diseases, asthma, birth defects, cerebrovascular health problems, global warming, rise in hospitalization, absenteeism in school, mortality, morbidity, and potential carcinogenicity (Bergstrom et al., 2017; Wang et al., 2018; Feng et al., 2019).

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Particulate matter causes haze affecting socio-economic developments in Addis Ababa
nodes.description: Additionally, particulate matter is a causative factor of haze in Addis Ababa, affecting the socio-economic developments, tourism sector, school activities, and cardiovascular system of both young and old (Song et al., 2019).

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Concentration of PM10 is rising in Addis Ababa due to emissions and urbanization
nodes.description: There has been a rise in the concentration of PM10 in Addis Ababa's atmosphere due to frequent vehicular emission, industrial emission, and urbanization (Althuwaynee et al., 2020).

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] High PM10 concentration in Addis Ababa requires quick intervention
nodes.description: Thus, the high concentration of ambient PM10 has been a major environmental concern that requires quick intervention.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Effective air quality management requires identifying PM10 hotspots in Addis Ababa
nodes.description: An essential aspect of effective air quality management is the ability to accurately identify potential hotspots of PM10 concentration for preemptive monitoring and timely control measures.

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Reliable PM10 hazard level estimation aids mitigation strategies in Addis Ababa
nodes.description: Reliable estimation of the PM10 hazard level will aid mitigation strategies by providing timely information to stakeholders, decision-makers, and government to curb the impacts of air pollution (Fortelli et al., 2016).

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Poor air quality in Addis Ababa is projected to increase due to unplanned growth
nodes.description: Considering that poor air quality is projected to increase due to unplanned population growth and socio-economic activities (She et al., 2017), studies that aim to advance ongoing initiatives to accurately predict future PM10 concentration leveraging advancements in geospatial technology and digitalization are essential.

nodes.description: However, recent studies that considered predicting PM10 concentration used a linear regression model (Abdullah et al. 2017, 2020; Fong et al., 2018).

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Machine learning techniques can overcome limitations of linear regression for PM10 prediction
nodes.description: One of the effective methods to overcome these limitations is machine learning techniques which are not restricted by the non-linear problem and the complexity of the relationship between the predictors and PM10 (Hu et al., 2017).

nodes.title: [2022-09-01 13:41] Machine learning models have shown high performance in predicting PM10 in other countries
nodes.description: Although limited studies use machine learning to predict PM10 in Addis Ababa, this approach has been widely utilized with high performance and accuracy in other countries.
